steps.title: Use subtraction to obtain 3x plus 3y
steps.content: Instead of solving for $x$ and $y$ individually, we can obtain $3x + 3y$ directly by subtracting Equation 2 from Equation 1. This works because $(7 - 4) = 3$ and $(-5 - (-8)) = 3$, so the $x$ and $y$ coefficients will both be $3$.

steps.title: Subtract the equations and simplify
steps.content:
Compute Equation 1 minus Equation 2:
$$(7x - 5y) - (4x - 8y) = 4 - 9$$
$$3x + 3y = -5$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
